sections.title: Key Risks
sections.content:
• Forward-looking statements involve risks and uncertainties that may cause actual results to differ materially. (Evidence: This Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q contains forward-looking statements... which may cause our actual results, performance, time frames or achievements to be materially different from any future result)
• NVIDIA's business is subject to risks detailed in the Annual Report on Form 10-K, which are incorporated by reference. (Evidence: We discuss many of these risks... in our Annual Report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended January 25, 2026 in greater detail under the heading 'Risk Factors'.)

sections.title: Financial Overview
sections.content:
• Revenue: $81.6B — Revenue for the three months ended April 26, 2026, per XBRL data. Prior period figures not available in provided excerpts.
• Net Income: $58.3B — Net income for the three months ended April 26, 2026, per XBRL data.
• Diluted EPS: $2.40 — Diluted earnings per share for the three months ended April 26, 2026, per XBRL data.
• Net Margin: 71.5% — Net margin calculated as net income divided by revenue for the current period.

sections.title: Management Commentary
sections.content:
Themes: - NVIDIA has evolved from PC graphics to a data center-scale AI infrastructure company.
- The company's platforms now address AI, data science, autonomous vehicles, robotics, and digital twins.
Quotes: - Speaker: Management (Item 1A Overview)
Quote: NVIDIA is now a data center-scale AI infrastructure company reshaping all industries.
Context: Describing the company's strategic transformation and current business focus.
Capital Allocation: - Not disclosed—no specific capital allocation details provided in excerpts.
Source Section Ref: Item 1A. Risk Factors (Overview)

summary.business_overview:
## Quarter at a Glance
- Revenue hit $81.6B for the three months ended April 26, 2026, per XBRL data.
- Net income reached $58.3B, yielding a net margin of 71.5%.
- Management describes NVIDIA as a data center-scale AI infrastructure company reshaping all industries.

## Key Numbers
- Revenue: $81.6B; prior period comparison not disclosed.
- Net income: $58.3B; prior period comparison not disclosed.
- Diluted EPS: $2.40; prior period comparison not disclosed.
- Net margin: 71.5%, calculated from current period figures.
- Cash flow and balance sheet figures were not disclosed in the provided excerpts.

## Guidance & Outlook
- No specific guidance figures were disclosed.
- Management’s commentary points to continued AI infrastructure demand as a general driver.

## Key Risks
- Forward-looking statements carry material uncertainty; actual results may differ significantly.
- Risks detailed in the Annual Report on Form 10-K for fiscal year ended January 25, 2026 remain relevant and are incorporated by reference.

## Liquidity & Balance Sheet
- Liquidity, leverage, and shareholder return figures were not disclosed.
- Debt covenants and contingent liabilities were not disclosed.

## Operational Highlights
- NVIDIA’s platforms now span AI, data science, autonomous vehicles, robotics, and digital twins.
- Segment-level revenue details were not disclosed.

writer.markdown:
## Quarter at a Glance
- Revenue hit $81.6B for the three months ended April 26, 2026, per XBRL data.
- Net income reached $58.3B, yielding a net margin of 71.5%.
- Management describes NVIDIA as a data center-scale AI infrastructure company reshaping all industries.

## Key Numbers
- Revenue: $81.6B; prior period comparison not disclosed.
- Net income: $58.3B; prior period comparison not disclosed.
- Diluted EPS: $2.40; prior period comparison not disclosed.
- Net margin: 71.5%, calculated from current period figures.
- Cash flow and balance sheet figures were not disclosed in the provided excerpts.

## Guidance & Outlook
- No specific guidance figures were disclosed.
- Management’s commentary points to continued AI infrastructure demand as a general driver.

## Key Risks
- Forward-looking statements carry material uncertainty; actual results may differ significantly.
- Risks detailed in the Annual Report on Form 10-K for fiscal year ended January 25, 2026 remain relevant and are incorporated by reference.

## Liquidity & Balance Sheet
- Liquidity, leverage, and shareholder return figures were not disclosed.
- Debt covenants and contingent liabilities were not disclosed.

## Operational Highlights
- NVIDIA’s platforms now span AI, data science, autonomous vehicles, robotics, and digital twins.
- Segment-level revenue details were not disclosed.
